summary: Process opened without substantive softening. Løkke's 10 April ultimatum forced V and KF into the negotiating format; Frederiksen on 12 April publicly adopted a broad-middle track in parallel with centre-left. But V (Lose, then TLP 13 April) still says S-led is 'ikke oplagt' and reframed fiscal responsibility as V's entry condition; SF (Dyhr 13 April) hard-vetoed governing with V or KF; RV (Lidegaard) stayed open both ways. Net: more procedural optionality, no reduction in substantive distance.

developments.description: Løkke ultimatum (10 April): Moderaterne stop attending unless Frederiksen widens talks to include V and KF. Frederiksen complies; V confirmed for Monday, KF for Tuesday.
developments.impact: M pulled V and KF into the format as a condition of M's continued participation — confirms active cross-bloc pull from M and broadens S's practical invitation set. Mapped to M→V inGov and S→V/KF inGov, not to crossBlocBonus (held stable to avoid double-counting last window's 5.0→6.0 bump).

developments.description: Stephanie Lose (V, 10 April) accepts Monday meeting but frames 'ansvarlig økonomisk politik' as Venstre's entry condition and says S-led govt still 'ikke oplagt'. Also urges Løkke to seek kongelig undersøger role for V 'med Troels for bordenden'.
developments.impact: V fiscal weight 0.75→0.85; V.participationPref.government 0.55→0.60, opposition 0.35→0.30 (attendance without convergence).

developments.description: Frederiksen (12 April) publicly opens broad-middle track: 'intensiverer vi drøftelserne også med Venstre og Konservative. Særligt om økonomi.' Keeps centre-left alive in parallel.
developments.impact: S→V inGov 0.25→0.35; S→KF inGov 0.30→0.40 (active invitation into govt talks, not just parliamentary support). S globalHarshness unchanged — both tracks remained open.

developments.description: Troels Lund Poulsen (V, 13 April morning) reiterates no shared economic foundation with S and argues new Folketing is 'mere blåt' and should produce a more blue government. Narrows the opening Løkke read into TLP's earlier outreach.
developments.impact: V globalHarshness held at 0.75 — no softening signal. V→S bilateral nudged up 0.32→0.35 reflecting procedural engagement only.

developments.description: Pia Olsen Dyhr (SF, 13 April) hard-vetoes governing with V or KF despite expanded table: 'SF kommer ikke til at gå i regering med Venstre eller De Konservative.' 'Alt for langt fra Venstre til SF.'
developments.impact: Reinforces existing SF→V and SF→KF near-zero inGov/tolerateInGov values (0.01–0.02). No numeric change; comment-only refresh to mark the signal is re-anchored, not stale.

developments.description: Martin Lidegaard (RV, 13 April): 'Vi skal have afklaring … vi er åbne for at forhandle begge veje.' Wants clarity 'inden for dage og ikke uger'.
developments.impact: RV→V inGov 0.22→0.30 — explicit two-track openness under S-led formation.

developments.description: M–SF track relatively deprioritized in Løkke's framing this window; Løkke's leverage move targeted expanding rightward rather than consolidating centre-left.

parameterChanges.justification: Løkke actively demanded V at the negotiating table on 10 April; the V–M personal-rivalry discount is weaker this window.
Løkke's ultimatum framed the centre-left-plus-M track as insufficient; M less willing to cabinet with SF as result.
V attended Monday's talks in Statsministeriet after Løkke's ultimatum — actual participation, not just availability.
Offsets the government-participation bump; V now procedurally engaged.
V went to the table but Lose and TLP both said S-led govt 'ikke oplagt'; small procedural bump only, no substantive acceptance.
Lose (10 April) framed 'ansvarlig økonomisk politik' as V's entry condition; TLP (13 April) reiterated no shared economic foundation. Fiscal elevated to public red line.
Lidegaard: 'åbne for at forhandle begge veje' under S-led formation. Explicit two-track openness.
Frederiksen (12 April) publicly invited V into government talks on broad-middle track, not only as parliamentary support.
Frederiksen same post: KF joined Tuesday's expanded govt talks.

confidenceAssessment: high on process moves (direct quotes from Løkke, Lose, Frederiksen, TLP, Dyhr, Lidegaard within window); medium on magnitudes — procedural openness is well-evidenced but substantive compatibility (SF↔V/KF, V↔S economic) remains blocked, so bumps on S→V/KF and M→V may widen the feasible-coalition set without producing passable ones. crossBlocBonus held stable to avoid double-counting 2026-04-09's 5.0→6.0 adjustment.
